questions.question_text: How many total hours of pre-license education must a Rhode Island salesperson candidate complete?
questions.explanation: Rhode Island salesperson candidates must complete 45 hours of DBR-approved coursework plus a mandatory 3-hour Lead Paint course, totaling 48 hours of pre-license education.

questions.question_text: A salesperson candidate wants to take the 45-hour pre-license course online at her own pace. Is this permitted in Rhode Island?
questions.correct_answer: No, distance learning is not allowed
distractors: Yes, if the provider is DBR-approved
Yes, with a proctored final exam only
Only if she completes it within 60 days
questions.explanation: Rhode Island requires pre-license education to be completed through in-person classroom instruction or monitored live broadcast. Self-paced online or distance learning formats are not permitted.

questions.question_text: How many hours of pre-licensing education are required for a Rhode Island broker candidate?
questions.explanation: Rhode Island broker candidates must complete 90 hours of pre-licensing education from approved providers. This is significantly more than the 45-hour salesperson requirement.

questions.question_text: A candidate completed the 45-hour salesperson course but skipped the Lead Paint module. What additional course must she complete before sitting for the exam?
questions.explanation: Rhode Island requires all salesperson candidates to complete a separate 3-hour Lead Paint course in addition to the 45-hour pre-license course. Both are mandatory before exam eligibility.

questions.question_text: A candidate completed the 45-hour pre-license salesperson course at a school that lost its DBR approval after the candidate enrolled. Is the candidate's coursework still valid?
questions.correct_answer: Only if done before approval was revoked
distractors: No, all coursework is automatically void
Only with a letter from the school
questions.explanation: Pre-license education must be completed through a DBR-approved provider. Coursework completed while the school was approved remains valid, but any coursework completed after approval is revoked would not count toward the requirement.

questions.question_text: What is the minimum age requirement to obtain a real estate license in Rhode Island?
questions.explanation: Applicants must be at least 18 years of age to obtain a real estate license in Rhode Island. This is consistent with the age requirement in most states.

questions.question_text: A license applicant submits her application with $140. The application is returned as incomplete. What additional fee is required?
questions.explanation: The total application fee is $165, consisting of $140 to the General Treasurer plus $25 for the Recovery Account. Both components must be included for a complete application.

questions.question_text: A candidate passes the Pearson VUE exam but does not activate her license for 20 months. Is her approved application still valid?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, applications remain valid for 24 months
distractors: No, applications expire after 12 months only
No, applications expire after just 6 months
Only if she retakes the licensing exam
questions.explanation: Approved license applications in Rhode Island remain valid for 24 months. Since only 20 months have passed, the candidate can still activate her license without reapplying.

questions.question_text: What minimum passing score is required on each section of the Rhode Island real estate licensing exam?
questions.explanation: Candidates must achieve a minimum score of 70% on each section of the Pearson VUE licensing exam. Both the national and state portions must be passed independently.

questions.question_text: An applicant has a prior criminal record. What document must she obtain as part of her Rhode Island license application?
questions.correct_answer: Criminal History Record from the AG Bureau
distractors: Court clearance letter from a local judge
Character reference from a licensed broker
questions.explanation: Rhode Island license applicants must obtain a Criminal History Record from the RI Attorney General's Bureau of Criminal Identification. This is a required part of the application process.

questions.question_text: When must Rhode Island real estate licenses be renewed?
questions.explanation: Rhode Island real estate licenses follow a biennial renewal cycle, with all licenses expiring on April 30 of even-numbered years. Licensees must complete renewal requirements before this deadline.

questions.question_text: How many hours of continuing education must a Rhode Island licensee complete each renewal cycle?
questions.explanation: Rhode Island licensees must complete 24 hours of continuing education every two years from approved providers. This aligns with the biennial renewal cycle ending April 30 of even-numbered years.

questions.question_text: A salesperson leaves Broker A and joins Broker B. What must the salesperson do regarding DBR?
questions.correct_answer: Notify DBR of the broker change
questions.explanation: Rhode Island licensees must notify DBR when changing brokers. This ensures that DBR records accurately reflect which broker is supervising each salesperson at all times.

questions.question_text: Effective June 2025, an agent holds both an MLO and RE license. What restriction applies when both roles overlap in a single transaction?
questions.correct_answer: Cannot collect compensation for both roles
distractors: Must disclose but can collect both fees
Must choose which license to surrender
Must have broker approval for dual pay
questions.explanation: Rhode Island's dual licensing prohibition effective June 2025 prevents a person holding both MLO and RE licenses from being compensated for both roles in the same transaction. This prevents conflicts of interest.

questions.question_text: A licensee forgot to renew by the April 30 deadline. What is the status of her license?
distractors: Automatically renewed with a late fee
Placed on inactive status for one year
questions.explanation: A Rhode Island real estate license that is not renewed by the April 30 deadline lapses. The licensee must then go through a reinstatement process to restore the license to active status.
